campaign: A3 — Autonomous /vision+/campaign drive for systemic escalations

phase_outcomes.P1: Drive-session skeleton. DriveRunner (mirror ImplementRunner) spawns a headless session that produces a vision .md + declares epicName+campaigns in PM_DRIVE_STATUS_PATH sentinel; finish() STRUCTURAL SEAL = file-on-disk cross-check (path-escape guard + fs.stat isFile) — vision_ready never inferred. LOOP does ALL PM I/O over HTTP (createEpic+createTask per campaign + addMessage(pendingDrive) acknowledged) because the worktree clone has no project-management MCP (.mcp.json gitignored — the defect that ESCALATED the first plan; resolved per the vision's commander-authority inline-orchestration option). Replaces the A1 systemic needs_human placeholder. Partial-failure (epic ok, task fails) → escalateToHuman naming the orphan epic, never stranded acknowledged. Reclaim skips pendingDrive. 151 responder tests (+30). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false. dc3e978.
phase_outcomes.P3: Checkpoint/resume (daemon-restart survival). The ONE real code change: an EARLY pre-epic intent marker in runDriveSession's vision_ready case — addMessage(escalationId, '<drive starting>', 'diagnosis', {pendingDrive:true, visionPath}) with NO epicId, written BEFORE createEpic; the existing terminal marker UPGRADES it with {epicId} after the tasks. This closes the duplicate-arc window: if the daemon dies AFTER createEpic but BEFORE the terminal marker, on restart the reclaim probe finds the intent marker (hasPendingDriveMarker) → routes to advanceArc → arcEpicId returns null (intent marker has no epicId) → the EXISTING null-epic branch (~1229) escalateToHuman recovers the rare orphan epic; NO second createEpic fires. arcEpicId-null→escalateToHuman was CONFIRMED already wired (no adjustment needed). Gaps 2-5 were already structurally closed (advanceArc derives arc state STRICTLY from the server each cycle — getEpic tasks + listMergeRequests(escalationId) — so a fresh createResponderState() is a faithful restart simulator; ResponderState is entirely transient); added SEALING tests, not code. The server-side markers + merge_requests ARE the checkpoint — NO progress.json checkpoint file, NO schema column, NO findArcEpicForEscalation api-client method. 7 new P3 tests (restart-resumes-next-phase, no-duplicate-epic-on-intent-marker, intent-marker-written-before-createEpic ordering via mock.invocationCallOrder, completed-arc-not-re-driven, no-double-submit-across-ticks, checkpoint-round-trips-fresh-state, byte-identical-disabled); 2 existing P1 tests updated (addMessage count now reflects the early intent marker: vision_ready writes 2 markers; createTask-fail-mid-loop writes the 1 intent marker before the orphan escalate). 168 responder tests (was 161 — +7 net). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false.
phase_outcomes.P2: Tick-driven campaign-phase drive (advanceArc), Model A. A self-held acknowledged escalation carrying pendingDrive/pendingArc is re-found by the reclaim pass each cycle and ROUTED to advanceArc (replacing P1's reclaim-SKIP of pendingDrive) at the marker-skip site. advanceArc derives arc state STRICTLY FROM THE SERVER — getEpic(campaign-tasks) + listMergeRequests(escalationId) matched by taskId — and does ONE of: re-park (a phase MR queued/integrating; one phase in flight max) | arc_partial (a phase MR rejected/abandoned/orphaned → escalateToHuman the ROOT naming landed shas + remaining; TERMINAL, no re-submit) | arc_complete (all landed → arcComplete marker, NOT resolve — P4's job; idempotent via hasArcCompleteMarker) | implement next phase (reuse runImplementForBranch: branch pm/escalation-<id>-<taskId>, phase brief, task-LINKED escalationId MR, pendingArc handoff). THE CRUX = Directive 1: gated BOTH land + reject post-backs in merge-request.service.ts on row.taskId===null so a phase MR threads escalationId for attribution WITHOUT an early phase resolving the root (only advanceArc drives completion/partial). Directive 2: advanceArc reserves spawn budget (canSpawn/recordSpawn) but NEVER reclaimAttempts (a >2-phase arc would poison-cap mid-drive). Refactored runImplementSession→shared runImplementForBranch(ImplementPhaseConfig); buildImplementPrompt gains optional PhaseBrief; api-client gains listMergeRequests + getEpic (epic + tasks via ?epic= list); merge-requests list route+service gain escalationId filter (openapi+web regen). 161 responder tests (+10), 1710 server tests (+3 Directive-1). A2 post-back tests stayed green (task-LESS bounded MR unaffected). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false.

notes: P2: extend the drive session to DRIVE the produced vision as a campaign — each phase runs plan→adversarial-verify→execute and lands via the SAME A2 verify-gated escalation-linked train MR (per-phase executor = A1 implement session; per-phase land = A2 submit→train→post-back, escalationId threaded). Result becomes arc_complete{landedShas[]}|arc_partial|give_up|error. A mid-campaign phase reject → arc_partial (proven phases preserved). Honor existing spawn/concurrency budget (A4 tightens). NOTE for P2 executor: P1 deliberately does NOT preserve the worktree vision .md (finally resets); the epic+tasks are the durable artifact — P2 owns whether to commit/regenerate the vision in-worktree for the campaign drive.
